skill.name: ceo-personal-os
skill.description: Build a private CEO personal operating system with executive reflection templates, goal reviews, leadership frameworks, and coaching-style interview prompts.
skill.summary: Create a structured reflection system for CEO planning, reviews, goals, and leadership decisions.
skill.author: 2389-research

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but review found the matches are ordinary markdown, business-framework language, formulas, directory trees, and citation links. No executable code, network fetch instructions, prompt injection attempts, or data exfiltration behavior were found. The skill does create local markdown files and may process sensitive executive reflections, so publication is acceptable with normal privacy caution.

low_findings.title: Local Filesystem Workflow Handles Sensitive Reflections
low_findings.description: The skill instructs the assistant to create a ceo-personal-os directory with markdown files, then summarize uploaded past reviews and append insights to memory.md. This is a legitimate productivity workflow, but it can store sensitive executive goals, personal reflections, and business context on the local filesystem.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The filesystem behavior is explicit in SKILL.md and is central to the skill design. I found no evidence that the data is sent to external services or hidden locations.

content.user_title: Build a CEO Personal Operating System
content.value_statement: Executives often have scattered goals, review notes, and decision frameworks. This skill creates a structured private system for reflection, planning, leadership review, and long-term pattern tracking.
actual_capabilities: Creates a markdown-based personal operating system structure for executive reflection.
Provides daily, weekly, quarterly, and annual review cadences.
Applies named leadership, customer development, time audit, and business design frameworks.
Runs coaching-style interviews for goals, stress patterns, people decisions, and venture health.
Summarizes uploaded past reviews and extracts recurring themes into a memory file.
Keeps the tone direct, calm, and executive-focused.
limitations: It is not a task manager or project management system.
It does not verify source frameworks against original books or current external sources.
It may handle sensitive personal and business information that users should store carefully.
It depends on the assistant to create and maintain markdown files correctly.

use_cases.title: Founder Annual Review
use_cases.description: Create a year-end reflection system that reviews wins, failures, relationships, energy, goals, and the story of the year.

use_cases.title: Executive Leadership Check-In
use_cases.description: Run quarterly leadership prompts about team health, strategic focus, customer learning, and failure risks.

use_cases.title: Chief of Staff Planning System
use_cases.description: Build reusable markdown templates for reviews, goals, frameworks, and uploaded review synthesis.

prompt_templates.title: Create My Personal OS
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for a new executive reflection system.
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me set up a CEO personal operating system for annual reviews, weekly reviews, goals, frameworks, and memory tracking.

prompt_templates.title: Run a Weekly Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Routine review after the system exists.
prompt_templates.prompt: Run my weekly CEO review. Focus on what moved the business forward, what created noise, time leaks, and one decision for next week.

prompt_templates.title: Assess Venture Risk
prompt_templates.scenario: Use startup failure and customer development frameworks.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Eisenmann and Blank frameworks to review our venture health. Identify failure patterns, search versus execute issues, and customer learning gaps.

prompt_templates.title: Synthesize Past Reviews
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced memory extraction from uploaded reflections.
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze my uploaded past reviews. Extract recurring goals, failure patterns, energy patterns, leadership themes, and insights to add to memory.md.

output_examples.input: Run my quarterly review as a founder who is unsure whether we are scaling too fast.
output_examples.output: A structured quarterly review with goal progress, failure-pattern checks, customer-development questions, team risks, and a short decision list.

output_examples.input: Help me design my 3-year vivid vision.
output_examples.output: A present-tense reflection covering professional role, relationships, health, finances, lifestyle, contribution, and decision filters.

output_examples.input: Summarize three uploaded annual reviews and find recurring patterns.
output_examples.output: A concise synthesis of repeated themes, avoided decisions, strengths, energy drains, and memory entries for future reviews.

best_practices: Start with the directory setup before asking for complex reviews.
Keep sensitive reflections in a private workspace with appropriate access controls.
Use quarterly and annual reviews to update goals and memory, not only to reflect.
anti_patterns: Do not use it as a daily task list or project tracker.
Do not upload confidential board, employee, or financial details unless storage is acceptable.
Do not treat coaching prompts as medical, legal, financial, or mental health advice.

faq.question: What does this skill build?
faq.answer: It builds a markdown-based personal operating system for CEO reflection, goals, reviews, frameworks, and memory tracking.

faq.question: Is this a task manager?
faq.answer: No. It is designed for reflection, pattern recognition, planning, and leadership review.

faq.question: Which frameworks does it include?
faq.answer: It references annual review, vivid vision, life map, coaching, failure patterns, Good to Great, Buy Back Your Time, E-Myth, customer development, and human-scale economics.

faq.question: Does it require internet access?
faq.answer: No. The reviewed files contain citation links, but no instruction to fetch network resources.

faq.question: What data should users protect?
faq.answer: Users should protect uploaded reviews, goals, leadership notes, business plans, and any personal reflections stored in the generated files.

faq.question: Can it analyze past reviews?
faq.answer: Yes. It can summarize uploaded review documents and extract recurring patterns into a memory file.
